$comment: LOCKED at F-008 commit a9e2344 (a9e23447bc5d8ea9c48ced55e916163ba2d21170). This policy schema is frozen. Any change requires a new $id (sentinel:policy:v2) and a migration plan — do not mutate this schema in place.
title: Sentinel Policy Intake
description: Policy records pushed DOWN into Sentinel by Delta via Anoryx-AI-Orchestrator (budget limits, model allow/deny lists). Every variant carries the four stable IDs from contracts/ids.md (LOCKED, IMMUTABLE) plus policy_id, policy_version, effective_from, and signature. NORMATIVE DISPATCH: variant selection is determined SOLELY by oneOf plus the policy_type const on each variant; the const values are unique and the variants are mutually exclusive, so exactly one matches a valid record. VALIDATOR DIALECT: Sentinel, Anoryx-AI-Orchestrator, and Delta MUST all validate these records with a JSON Schema Draft 2020-12 validator so no parser-differential exists. All objects are closed (additionalProperties:false) and all fields bounded, per the F-001 audit findings. SECURITY (cross-tenant poisoning defense): the body IDs (tenant_id/team_id/project_id/agent_id) are NOT authoritative. The gateway MUST resolve the authoritative scope SERVER-SIDE from the verified policy 'signature' (F-008) and REJECT any record whose body IDs disagree with the signature-resolved scope. This mirrors the virtual-API-key key-to-ID binding and id_context_mismatch rejection in contracts/openapi.yaml (ADR-0002): supplied IDs are a cross-check only and can never widen or impersonate a scope the signature does not authorize. SECURITY (replay/rollback defense): policy_version is monotonic per policy_id; intake MUST reject any record whose policy_version is <= the currently stored version for the same policy_id, so an attacker cannot re-submit an older signed policy to roll enforcement back. F-008 SCOPE: runtime cryptographic signature verification and the scope-resolve-and-reject obligation are implemented in F-008; THIS SCHEMA enforces only the PRESENCE and FORMAT of the signature field, not its validity. Full lock of this contract is gated on F-008 landing.
$comment: Dispatch is normative via oneOf + the policy_type const on each variant under JSON Schema Draft 2020-12. The 'discriminator' keyword below is a NON-NORMATIVE hint for OpenAPI tooling ONLY; it is not a 2020-12 keyword and has NO validation effect under this dialect. Do not rely on it for correctness.

tenant_id.description: Stable tenant identifier (UUID v4). LOCKED/IMMUTABLE per contracts/ids.md. NOT AUTHORITATIVE / cross-check ONLY: the gateway resolves the authoritative tenant from the verified signature server-side (F-008) and rejects any record whose body tenant_id disagrees with the signature-resolved scope.

team_id.description: Stable team identifier (UUID v4). LOCKED/IMMUTABLE per contracts/ids.md. Cross-check only; authoritative value resolved from signature server-side.

project_id.description: Stable project identifier (UUID v4). LOCKED/IMMUTABLE per contracts/ids.md. Cross-check only; authoritative value resolved from signature server-side.

agent_id.description: Internal Sentinel component name (lowercase slug). LOCKED/IMMUTABLE per contracts/ids.md.

policy_id.description: Stable policy identifier (UUID v4). Joins to policy_id on policy_violated events in contracts/events.schema.json.

policy_version.description: Monotonic version counter PER policy_id (policy_id is the monotonicity target key). Intake MUST reject a record whose policy_version is <= the currently stored version for the same policy_id (replay/rollback defense). Bounded to 9007199254740991 (JS Number.MAX_SAFE_INTEGER) to avoid IEEE-754 precision loss in JSON consumers. The schema enforces only the integer bound; monotonicity itself is a runtime hint until F-008 enforces it against stored state.

effective_from.description: When this policy begins to apply (RFC 3339 / ISO 8601 date-time, UTC).

signature.description: Compact-JWS cryptographic signature over the policy record (three dot-separated base64url segments), produced by Delta/Orchestrator. The body IDs (tenant_id/team_id/project_id/agent_id) are NOT authoritative: the gateway MUST resolve the authoritative tenant/team/project scope from this VERIFIED signature SERVER-SIDE and REJECT any record whose body IDs disagree with the signature-resolved scope (cross-tenant poisoning defense; mirrors contracts/openapi.yaml virtual-API-key key-to-ID binding and id_context_mismatch). SCHEMA ENFORCES PRESENCE + FORMAT ONLY (minLength 16, compact-JWS shape); a syntactically valid signature is NOT a verified signature. Runtime cryptographic verification of validity and the scope-resolve-and-reject obligation land in F-008; full lock of this contract is gated on F-008 landing. A present, well-formed, but cryptographically invalid signature MUST be rejected at runtime, not accepted because the schema validated.

BudgetLimitPolicy.description: Budget cap enforced at the gateway. Supports the Delta budget-enforcement path (throttle over-budget agents). max_cost_cents_per_period is a client-side cost estimate basis, not an authoritative bill. SCHEMA-ENFORCED: at least one of max_tokens_per_period or max_cost_cents_per_period MUST be present (see anyOf), so a budget that limits nothing fails validation.

max_tokens_per_period.description: Maximum total tokens allowed in the period. At least one of max_tokens_per_period or max_cost_cents_per_period MUST be present (enforced by the object-level anyOf); a budget limiting neither fails validation.

max_cost_cents_per_period.description: Maximum client-side cost estimate (cents) allowed in the period. Estimate basis, not an authoritative bill.

scope.description: Granularity at which the budget is enforced.

ModelAllowlistPolicy.description: Allow-list of model IDs permitted within the signed scope. Filters GET /v1/models and gates request routing in contracts/openapi.yaml.

allowed_model_ids.description: Model IDs explicitly permitted. Bounded to 512 entries to cap intake cost. An allowlist policy MUST specify at least one allowed model. To block all models, use ModelDenylistPolicy with the catch-all denial pattern, or set budget_limit to zero.

effective_until.description: OPTIONAL expiry of the allow-list (RFC 3339 / ISO 8601 date-time, UTC). Omit for no expiry.

ModelDenylistPolicy.description: Deny-list of model IDs forbidden within the signed scope. Deny takes precedence over allow at enforcement time.

denied_model_ids.description: Model IDs explicitly forbidden. Bounded to 512 entries to cap intake cost.

reason.description: Human-readable rationale for the denial (for audit-ready evidence).

CodeScanPolicy.description: Per-tenant code-scanning posture (F-016, ADR-0019 §9). Enables the post-response code-scan detector within the signed scope and maps scanner finding severity onto the PASS|WARN|BLOCK verdict and a per-verdict action. Default-OFF, per-tenant opt-in (ADR-0019 Fork 4): an absent code_scan policy means scanning is disabled. The detector reads the stored policy_payload via the F-008 policy engine (src/code_scan/config.py::_parse_payload); this schema mirrors the payload shape that loader reads (enabled, thresholds.warn/block, actions.warn/block). HONEST-LANGUAGE / HONEST-SCOPE (ADR-0019 §4): a BLOCK verdict only rejects NON-STREAMED responses; for streamed responses the bytes are already on the wire, so the verdict degrades to WARN + audit (no honest block is possible). The scanner fails safe to WARN on any error/timeout. This is high-coverage detection of known patterns and risk reduction, not a guarantee that passing code is safe.

enabled.description: Master switch for code scanning within the signed scope. false (or an absent policy) disables the detector cheaply with no scanner invocation (default-OFF, ADR-0019 Fork 4).

thresholds.description: Severity boundaries (inclusive) that map the worst scanner finding onto the verdict. A finding whose severity is >= warn yields WARN; >= block yields BLOCK. block SHOULD be >= warn; severity order is low < medium < high < critical (ordering is enforced at runtime, not by this schema).

warn.description: Minimum finding severity (inclusive) that yields a WARN verdict.

block.description: Minimum finding severity (inclusive) that yields a BLOCK verdict (non-streamed only; see HONEST-SCOPE).

actions.description: Per-verdict action mapping. "audit" records an event and lets the response pass unchanged; "reject" rejects a non-streamed response via the existing policy_blocked (403) envelope (no new error_code, ADR-0019 §8).

warn.description: Action for a WARN verdict. Normally "audit" (observe only).

block.description: Action for a BLOCK verdict. "reject" enables a real block on non-streamed responses; "audit" downgrades even a BLOCK-threshold verdict to WARN + audit for tenants that want signal without rejection (ADR-0019 §9).

DataLockPolicy.description: Per-tenant JSON data-lock posture (F-017, ADR-0020 §9). Enables the post-response data-lock detector within the signed scope. The detector evaluates each rule against a field in the LLM response identified by a dotted field_path; if the rule's release condition is not met the field VALUE is withheld (field_locked / lock_condition_denied), otherwise it is released unchanged (field_unlocked). Default-OFF, per-tenant opt-in: an absent data_lock policy means data-locking is disabled. The detector reads the stored policy_payload via the F-008 policy engine; this schema mirrors the payload shape that loader reads (enabled, rules[].field_path, rules[].condition). FAIL-CLOSED (ADR-0020 §6): if the ruleset is unevaluable the whole response is blocked (data_lock_error). V1 SUPPORTS ONLY the 'time' and 'permission' condition types; an approval-based condition is DEFERRED. This is high-coverage field-level gating of known JSON shapes and risk reduction, not a guarantee that an unlocked field is safe to release.

enabled.description: Master switch for data-locking within the signed scope. false (or an absent policy) disables the detector cheaply with no field traversal (default-OFF).

items.description: One data-lock rule: a field path plus the release condition gating that field's value.

field_path.description: Dotted path to the locked field within the JSON response (e.g. 'result.ssn', 'a.b[].c' where '[]' matches each element of an array). DIALECT (bounded dotted-path, NOT JSONPath; mirrors src/data_lock/selector.py::parse_path): each '.'-separated segment is one or more chars of [A-Za-z0-9_-] optionally followed by '[]' (array wildcard); empty segments ('a..b'), leading/trailing dots, and other characters ('a.$x') are rejected. Bounded to <=16 segments by the pattern's {0,15} quantifier so a contract-valid path never exceeds the runtime MAX_PATH_SEGMENTS cap (R7) and is always processable (no fail-closed self-DoS for an over-long path). maxLength 256 is a backstop. The detector still traverses under a bounded traversal-node budget; on budget exhaustion it fails closed (data_lock_error / 'traversal_budget'). A field LOCATION only — never a field value.

condition.description: Release condition gating the field value. V1 supports ONLY 'time' and 'permission' (approval-based conditions are deferred).

oneOf.description: Time lock: the field value is withheld until unlock_at, then released.

unlock_at.description: When the field unlocks (RFC 3339 / ISO 8601 date-time, UTC). Before this instant the field value is withheld (field_locked); at or after it the value is released (field_unlocked).

oneOf.description: Permission lock: the field value is released ONLY when the caller's scope matches the allow-list; otherwise it is withheld (lock_condition_denied). An allow array that is present and non-empty constrains that dimension; an omitted dimension is unconstrained.

allow.description: Allow-list of caller scopes permitted to receive the field value. At least one dimension SHOULD be specified; an empty object permits no caller (ordering/emptiness semantics are enforced at runtime, not by this schema).

team_id.description: OPTIONAL allow-list of team_id values (UUID v4) permitted to receive the field value. Bounded to 256 entries to match the runtime cap (src/data_lock/conditions.py::_MAX_ALLOW_VALUES_PER_ATTR, R7); a contract-valid allow-list is always processable and never fail-closed-blocked for exceeding the runtime cap.

project_id.description: OPTIONAL allow-list of project_id values (UUID v4) permitted to receive the field value. Bounded to 256 entries to match the runtime cap (src/data_lock/conditions.py::_MAX_ALLOW_VALUES_PER_ATTR, R7); a contract-valid allow-list is always processable and never fail-closed-blocked for exceeding the runtime cap.

agent_id.description: OPTIONAL allow-list of agent_id slugs permitted to receive the field value. Bounded to 256 entries to match the runtime cap (src/data_lock/conditions.py::_MAX_ALLOW_VALUES_PER_ATTR, R7); a contract-valid allow-list is always processable and never fail-closed-blocked for exceeding the runtime cap.

rules.description: Data-lock rules evaluated against the response. Bounded to 100 entries to cap intake cost.
